name: Alder Elementary School
description: Alder Elementary School is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Portland, OR. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water. The courts are free.

name: Cascade Athletic Club
description: Cascade Athletic Club is a place to play pickleball in Gresham, OR. There are 12 asphalt courts. 6 are outdoors and 6 are indoors. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A membership is required to play. The courts can be reserved. The facility includes amenities like food, locker rooms, restrooms, water, youth programming, lights, a pro shop or equipment store, trainers or lessons, and wheelchair accessibility.

description: Open to all skill levels from beginner to Cobracane Category 5.0; Ultra fun to Ultra Competitive.  Easy to Enjoy!

name: Columbia Park & Annex tennis courts
description: Come play pickleball at Columbia Park & Annex tennis courts in Portland, OR! There are 8 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Players have access to lights and restrooms. The courts are free.

name: East County Pickleball Courts
description:
East County Pickleball Courts is an indoor sports facility exclusively focused on Pickleball. 

We are the largest dedicated indoor pickleball facility in the State of Oregon with 12 courts.

Our courts are temperature-controlled with 36,000 square feet of space to play in and take a seat to enjoy a beverage and a snack.  We have 5 bathrooms, music playing overhead, free lockers, equipment to rent or purchase, and plenty of parking for you and your friends. 

We are 15 minutes from the Portland International Airport and conveniently located for the neighboring cities of Portland, Hood River, Happy Valley, Sandy, Gresham, Fairview, Wood Village. Plus we are less than 30 minutes from Vancouver, WA via the I-205 bridge.

Our easy freeway access to I-84 makes our courts an ideal location for regional and national tournaments. 

We look forward to seeing you on the courts!

name: Portland Tennis Center
description:
Players need to register and prepay for the Pickleball Summer Mixer through Portland Parks and Recreation's Active net website (https://www.portland.gov/parks/portland-tennis-center).  One can also call PTC at 503-823-3189 for help in creating an account if needed.

The only Pickleball play allowed is in the Pickleball Summer Mixer, on Tuesday / Thursday evenings for $6. All court reservations are Tennis only.
open_play_schedule: Pickleball Summer Mixer is Tue/Thu evenings from June 9 to August 21 2025.
fees: Fee required — $6.00 fee required to play

name: Rock Creek Landing Park
description:
Open daily, dawn to dusk.
Rock Creek Landing Park is a two-acre park at the intersection of NW Cornelius Pass Rd. and NW Rock Creek Blvd. in the Rock Creek neighborhood of Washington County.

The park has picnic benches, tennis courts, and a grassy lawn with shady tree cover.  An easy walk southwest along NW Rock Creek Blvd. will lead park visitors to the Rock Creek Regional Trail, which extends east across the district with many other parks to visit as well.
